nodes.description: Oral azithromycin is preferred for treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Yaws - A Review of Clinical Features, Diagnosis and Treatment.
evidence.snippet: Though yaws can be treated with benzathine penicillin, oral azithromycin has become the preferred treatment due to its ease of administration.

nodes.description: Treponema pallidum subsp. pertenue depends on 70S-ribosome translation. Azithromycin, a macrolide and the preferred oral treatment for yaws, binds the 50S subunit and blocks the polypeptide exit tunnel, arresting bacterial protein synthesis. (Benzathine penicillin, a PBP/cell-wall agent, is the alternative.)

evidence.snippet: The ribosome is one of the main antibiotic targets in the bacterial cell.
